LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PILAR GRAHAM'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S DECLARATIONS DIRECTLY CONTRADICT PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY AS TO WHAT SHE TOLD THEM ABOUT HER ALLEGED DISABILITIES. MOREOVER, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION CANNOT BE GRANTED ON THE LIABILITY ISSUE ALONE. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ESTABLISH THE AMOUNT OF HER DAMAGES. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

**(CONTINUATION OF RULING RE DEFENDANT CHARLES & SCHWAB & CO., INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT) PLAINTIFF RELIES ON TEMPORAL PROXIMITY FROM WHEN SHE FAINTED TO WHEN SHE WAS PLACED ON THE ACTION PLAN. THIS, STANDING ALONE, IS NOT ENOUGH TO REBUT AN EMPLOYER'S EVIDENCE OF A LEGITIMATE BUSINESS PURPOSE. ARTEAGA V. BRINK'S, INC. (2008) 163 CA 4TH 327. ACCORDINGLY, THE WRONGFUL DISCHARGE CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS FOR THE SAME REASON THE DISCRIMINATION CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS. BECAUSE THERE IS NO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION IN THE ABSENCE OF ACTUAL DISCRIMINATION, THIS CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS AS WELL. TRUJILO V. N. COUNTY TRANSIT DIST. (1998) 63 C.A. 4TH 280. DEFENDANT IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

ORDER ON PLAINTIFF PILAR GRAHAM'S OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHARLES & SCHWAB & CO., INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT RULED ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE. ORDER SIGNED. SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. ------------------------------------------------------- AFTER THE HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. AN ESSENTIAL ELEMENT OF BOTH THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PROVIDE A REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION AND A DISCRIMINATION CAUSE OF ACTION IS THAT DEFENDANT KNEW PLAINTIFF HAD DISABILITY AS DEFINED UNDER FEHA. DEFENDANT HAS SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT EXISTS AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF INFORMED DEFENDANT OF A DISABILITY AS UNDERSTOOD BY FEHA. PENSINGER V. BOWSMITH (1998) 60 CA 4TH 709. FURTHERMORE, THERE IS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT FAILED TO ACCOMMODATE PLAINTIFF'S HEART CONDITION OR SUBJECTED PLAINTIFF TO AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION DUE TO THE SAME. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ON THIS ISSUE IS INSUFFICIENT TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. AS FOR THE DISCRIMINATION CLAIM, DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCTION OF EVIDENCE BY PRESENTING EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS NOT SUBJECTED TO AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF WAS TOLD SHE HAD THE OPTION OF COMPLYING WITH TERMS OF THE ACTION PLANS, BUT INSTEAD CHOSE TO QUIT. THE PLAN ITSELF AND THE POOR PERFORMANCE REVIEW ARE NOT ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS. YANOWITZ V. L'OREAL (2005) 36 CAL 4TH 1028. WHILE PLAINTIFF DOES CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER SHE WAS FORCED OUT AS OPPOSED TO VOLUNTARILY QUITTING, SHE HAS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THIS WAS CONNECTED TO HER CLAIMED DISABILITIES AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT'S LEGITIMATE BUSINESS REASONS (PLAINTIFF'S POOR PERFORMANCE) WERE PRETEXTUAL. (SEE CONTINUATION OF RULING.**)

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PILAR GRAHAM'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Barbara Figari and Collin Moore (235.2100) for plaintiff and moving party; David J. Reis and Emily J. Leahy of Howard Rice (434.1600) for defendant and responding party.
This matter was not argued. The count adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the cause of action for Failure to Accommodate is denied. Defendant's declarations directly contradict plaintiff's testimony as to what she told them about her alleged disabilities. Moreover, motion for summary adjudication cannot be granted on the liability issue alone. Plaintiff failed to establish the amount of her damages. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Defendant Charles & Schwab & Co., Inc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHARLES & SCHWAB & CO., INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Appearances: Barbara Figari and Collin Moore (235.2100) for plaintiff and responding party; David J. Reis and Emily J. Leahy of Howard Rice (434.1600) for defendant and moving party.

The court ruled on plaintiff's objections to defendant's evidence. Order signed. Summary judgment was argued and the court took the matter under submission.
-------------------------------------------------------
After the hearing, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. An essential element of both the cause of action for failure to provide a reasonable accommodation and a discrimination cause of action is that defendant knew plaintiff had disability as defined under FEHA. Defendant has satisfied its burden of demonstrating that no triable issue of fact exists as to whether plaintiff informed defendant of a disability as understood by FEHA. Pensinger v. Bowsmith (1998) 60 CA 4th 709. Furthermore, there is insufficient evidence that defendant failed to accommodate plaintiff's heart condition or subjected plaintiff to an adverse employment action due to the same. Plaintiff's evidence on this issue is insufficient to create a triable issue of fact. As for the discrimination claim, defendant shifted the burden of production of evidence by presenting evidence that plaintiff was not subjected to an adverse employment action because plaintiff was told she had the option of complying with terms of the action plans, but instead chose to quit. The plan itself and the poor performance review are not adverse employment actions. Yanowitz v. L'Oreal (2005) 36 Cal 4th 1028. While plaintiff does create a triable issue of fact as to whether she was forced out as opposed to voluntarily quitting, she has insufficient evidence that this was connected to her claimed disabilities and plaintiff does not create a triable issue of material fact as to whether defendant's legitimate business reasons (plaintiff's poor performance) were pretextual. Plaintiff relies on temporal proximity from when she fainted to when she was placed on the action plan. This, standing alone, is not enough to rebut an employer's evidence of a legitimate business purpose. Arteaga v. Brink's, Inc. (2008) 163 CA 4th 327. Accordingly, the wrongful discharge cause of action fails for the same reason the discrimination cause of action fails. Because there is no cause of action for failure to prevent discrimination in the absence of actual discrimination, this cause of action fails as well. Trujilo v. N. County Transit Dist. (1998) 63 C.A. 4th 280. Defendant is directed to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
